name: Qase Test Case Upload with Slack Notification

parameters.content:
## Qase Test Case Upload with Slack Notification

### How it works

This workflow accepts a form submission containing a document URL, downloads the referenced CSV, and extracts its contents. It maps the CSV data into the Qase project/workspace format, uploads the resulting test cases to Qase, then aggregates the upload results. Finally, it posts a summary notification to Slack.

### Setup steps

- Configure the form trigger to collect the required Document URL field.
- Ensure the document URL is accessible to the HTTP request node and points to a CSV-compatible file.
- Set up the Qase API request with the correct API token, project code, workspace mapping, and endpoint details.
- Configure the Slack notification HTTP request with the correct webhook URL or Slack API credentials.

### Customization

Adjust the QaseMapping code node to match your CSV column names, Qase project codes, workspace rules, and desired test case fields. Update the Slack message payload to include the summary details your team wants to see.

## Collect CSV input

Starts from a form submission, downloads the document from the submitted URL, and extracts the CSV file contents for processing.

## Map and upload cases

Transforms the extracted CSV rows into the project and workspace structure expected by Qase, then posts the generated test cases to the Qase API.

## Summarize and notify

Aggregates the upload results and sends a Slack notification with the outcome.

json.title: Successful User Login with Valid Credentials
json.description: Verify that a registered user can successfully log in using a valid email and password.
json.preconditions: User is registered; User is on the login page.
json.postconditions: User is redirected to the dashboard; Session token is generated.

json.title: Login Failure with Incorrect Password
json.description: Verify that an error message is displayed when a registered user attempts to log in with an incorrect password.
json.preconditions: User is registered; User is on the login page.
json.postconditions: Error message "Invalid credentials" is displayed; User remains on the login page.

json.title: Password Field Boundary Value - Minimum Characters
json.description: Verify that the system accepts a password exactly at the minimum allowable limit of 8 characters during registration.
json.preconditions: User is on the registration page.
json.postconditions: Account is successfully created; User is logged in.

json.title: Unauthorized API Access to User Profile
json.description: Verify that an unauthenticated API request to fetch user profile details returns a 401 Unauthorized status code.
json.preconditions: API endpoint is active; Request does not include a Bearer token.
json.postconditions: System returns 401 Unauthorized error response; No user data is leaked.

json.title: Checkout Session Timeout Handling
json.description: Verify that the system cancels the checkout process and releases reserved inventory if the session expires due to inactivity.
json.preconditions: User has items in cart; User is on the checkout page; Session timer is set to 1 second remaining.
json.postconditions: Session expires; Items are returned to inventory; User is redirected to cart with a timeout message.
